---
title: "VOCs"
author: "Zulema Carracedo Lorenzo"
date: "2024-06-10"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Load packages

```{r}
#data management
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readxl)

#statistics
library(multcompView)
library(vegan)
library(pairwiseAdonis)

#plots
library(ggplot2)

#for exporting tables 
library(flextable)
library(officer)
```

#Code from Roland

##Import data

```{r}


#load metadata

# load csv with metadata (trap ID and strain) 
vocs_metadata <- read_excel("2_Pseudomonas_GC-MS/data/metadata.xlsx")

# Use trap id as row index
vocs_metadata <- data.frame(vocs_metadata)
rownames(vocs_metadata) <- vocs_metadata$ID

# Remove explicit trap id column
vocs_metadata <- subset(vocs_metadata, select = strain)

#load GC-MS data

gcms_data <- read_csv("2_Pseudomonas_GC-MS/data/with_blanck_transfrmed.csv")
gcms_data <- data.frame(gcms_data) 
rownames(gcms_data) <- gcms_data$...1
gcms_data <- gcms_data[, -1]  #remove first colum
gcms_data <- t(gcms_data) #trap ID to rowname
gcms_data <- data.frame(gcms_data)

str(vocs_metadata)
str(gcms_data)

rownames(vocs_metadata)
rownames(gcms_data)

```

##PCA calculation

```{r}

# join metadata and GC-MS data

#i need to have rownames as columns for that
vocs_metadata <- rownames_to_column(vocs_metadata, var= "ID")
gcms_data <- rownames_to_column(gcms_data, var= "ID")

vocs_pca <- merge(vocs_metadata, gcms_data, by = "ID")
vocs_pca <- vocs_pca[,-1]

# calculate PCA
pca_res <- prcomp(vocs_pca[, 2:ncol(vocs_pca)], scale. = TRUE) # "scale. = TRUE" indicates that the variables should be scaled to have unit variance before calculating principle components. we start at column 2 to exclude the metadata columns

# make data frame with pseudomonas strain and PCs
PCi <- data.frame(pca_res$x, strain=vocs_pca$strain)

# extract proportion of variance explained by each PC
pca_prop_variance <- summary(pca_res)$importance["Proportion of Variance",]
# change it to percentages and round to 1 decimal
pca_prop_variance <- round(pca_prop_variance*100, digits=1)
  
```

##PCA plot

```{r}

PCi$strain <- factor(PCi$strain, c("Control", "WCS417", "WCS315", "CHA0")) #order matters for plots

PCA <- ggplot(PCi,aes(x=PC1,y=PC2,color=strain))+
  geom_point(size=8)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#76CFE8", "#C9DC53", "#F99F58", "#9771A5"))+
  xlab(paste0("PC1 [", pca_prop_variance["PC1"], "%]"))+ # add percent variance explained by PC1 on x-axis label
  ylab(paste0("PC2 [", pca_prop_variance["PC2"], "%]")) + # add percent variance explained by PC2 on y-axis label
   guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  #guides(color=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21))) +
  #guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21,fill=c("#FFFF99","#A6CEE3","#B2DF8A","#CAB2D6"),color=c("#1F78B4","#E31A1C")))) +
  theme_bw(18) +
  labs(color = "Pseudomonas strain",) +
  #guides(fill= "none") +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank()) 

ggsave("PCA.pdf", width = 40, height = 20, units = "cm", dpi=800)

mapply(function(x)
  ggsave(
  plot = last_plot(),
  filename =x,
  path = "./2_Pseudomonas_GC-MS/results/plots",
  scale = 1,
  width = 40,
  height = 20,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 300),
x=c("Fig.1b_PC.svg","Fig.1b_PC.png"))
 

```

## calculate metabolome dissimilarities between samples

```{r}
#the trap ID must be in the same order in the euclidean distances than in the vocs_metadata dataframe
vocs_metadata <- rownames_to_column(vocs_metadata, var= "ID")
gcms_data <- rownames_to_column(gcms_data, var= "ID")

merged_data <- merge(vocs_metadata, gcms_data, by = "ID")


#now split again to have them i the same ID order
vocs_metadata <- merged_data[, 1:2]
gcms_data <- merged_data
gcms_data <- column_to_rownames(gcms_data, var = "ID")
gcms_data <- gcms_data[,-1]

# now the orher is the same for both dataframes


# Euclidean distances (Bray curtis can't be used when negative values)

euclidean_dist <- vegdist(gcms_data, method = "euclidean")

```

##Permanova

```{r}
#the trap ID must be in the same order in the euclidean distances than in the vocs_metadata dataframe

#Order was arrange in the previous chunk, see lines 126-136

#Run permanova with adonis2
set.seed(3848)
permanova <- adonis2(euclidean_dist ~ strain, 
        data = vocs_metadata,
        permutations = 9999)

#save output 
# Convert output to data frame
permanova_df <- as.data.frame(permanova)
permanova_df$Factor <- rownames(permanova_df)
permanova_df <- permanova_df[, c("Factor", setdiff(names(permanova_df), "Factor"))]

# Round values
permanova_df$`Pr(>F)` <- formatC(permanova_df$`Pr(>F)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
permanova_df$R2 <- round(permanova_df$R2, 3)
permanova_df$F <- round(permanova_df$F, 2)
permanova_df$SumOfSqs <- round(permanova_df$SumOfSqs, 2)

# Create styled flextable
permanova_ft <- flextable(permanova_df) |>
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ `Pr(>F)` < 0.05, part = "body") |>
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor", Df = "DF", SumOfSqs = "Sum Sq",
    R2 = "R²", F = "F-value", `Pr(>F)` = "p-value"
  )

# Export to Word
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table: PERMANOVA Results (Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity)", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(permanova_ft)

print(doc, target = "PERMANOVA.docx")

#pairwise adonis

pairwise_results<-pairwise.adonis2(euclidean_dist ~ strain, data = vocs_metadata)

#save table for publication 

# Convert results to data frame
pairwise_df <- do.call(rbind, pairwise_results[-1])  # skip $parent_call
pairwise_df$Comparison <- names(pairwise_results)[-1]
pairwise_df <- pairwise_df[, c("Comparison", names(pairwise_df)[1:(ncol(pairwise_df)-1)])]

# Make table
pairwise_ft <- flextable(pairwise_df) |>
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ `Pr(>F)` < 0.05, part = "body") |>
  set_header_labels(
    Comparison = "Comparison",
    Df = "DF",
    SumOfSqs = "Sum Sq",
    R2 = "R²",
    F = "F-value",
    `Pr(>F)` = "p-value"
  )

# Export
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table: Pairwise PERMANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(pairwise_ft)

print(doc, target = "pairwise_PERMANOVA.docx")



```
